title: VETERINARY CARE - 211info
text: PRO-BONE-O VETERINARY CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Volunteer-run clinics provide medical exams, vaccinations, flea treatments, and vouchers for spay/neuter services. Services are provided on a first-come, first-serve basis, and individuals are limited to two pets per person. Assistance is dependent upon available abilities and not guaranteed. 1509 W 1st Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Individuals who are homeless Hours: 2nd and 4th Sundays of the month, 9am-1pm. Gates open at 8:45am and the last patients will be admitted at noon. Email (541) 799-0441 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Message Phone (541) 799-0441 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Wait outside of the gates for instructions to enter from the volunteer FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English AREA SERVED: Lane County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Go to the end of the street. Clinic is in the big red warehouse on the right side of 1st Ave. Signs at the Eugene Mission lead to the clinic's location in the warehouse. Services: Veterinary Care Expense Assistance * Homelessness Issues Well Animal Checkups * Homelessness Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: VOLUNTEER OPPORTUNITIES - VOLUNTEERS AND DONATIONS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. PRO-BONE-O
